title: onopordum acanthium subsp. gautieri
intro: Onopordum acanthium subsp. gautieri, also known as Cotton Thistle, is a subspecies of thistle native to the Iberian Peninsula and France. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, making it beneficial for treating conditions such as kidney stones and urinary tract infections. The plant's medicinal properties also provide health benefits like reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Cotton Thistle include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other topical applications for its medicinal effects.
